angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Toccoa River offers fair to good trout fishing with steady flow and recent stocking supporting active fish. Water temperature is unavailable, but current conditions and light suggest a balanced nymph approach.
generated_text.best_window: Day 1 morning is best due to stable light with 55% sky cover and a balanced pressure reading, promoting comfortable trout activity near runs, seams, and structure for effective nymph fishing.
generated_text.current_conditions: Flow is steady at 233 cfs with a gage height of 1.22 ft; water temperature and turbidity data are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover ranges from 13% to 55% averaging 31%, pressure is 30.08 inHg with no clear trend, light wind averages 3 mph with gusts up to 11.5 mph, and rain chances are low with 0.0 in expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout likely hold in runs, seams, and near structure, favoring comfortable feeding lanes under moderate light. Present clean drifts with smaller natural flies and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell nymphs for best results.
